title: Carex potosina
taxonomy: Carex potosina is classified within the family Cyperaceae, which is a group of monocotyledonous flowering plants commonly known as sedges. This classification is based on its unique characteristics, such as its jointed stem and flat, overlapping leaves. Carex potosina is further classified within the genus Carex, which comprises about 2,000 species of sedges. The genus Carex is a member of the tribe Cariceae, which is a group of sedge species that share similar characteristics. Carex potosina is native to the Americas and is often found in wetlands and moist areas.
